description: The Hells Angels Motorcycle Club is an international outlaw biker gang that expanded to Australia in 1975, establishing chapters in Melbourne and Sydney. It has approximately 250 members and 14 chapters in Australia, involved in drug trafficking, violent crimes, and money laundering through legitimate businesses. The club is legally classified as a criminal organization in Queensland and has been involved in conflicts with rival gangs such as the Comancheros and Bandidos.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Hells Angels expanded to Australia in 1975, initially establishing chapters in Melbourne and Sydney, and now have approximately 250 members and 14 chapters in the country.
country_links.context: The Hells Angels expanded to Australia in 1975, establishing chapters and engaging in drug trafficking, violent crimes, and other activities.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 1980, Melbourne chapter founding members Peter John Hill and Raymond Hamment flew to California to visit Oakland chapter president Kenny Walton in prison... the Australians supplied the Oakland chapter with 300 litres of the chemical phenylacetone
country_links.context: The Hells Angels' Melbourne chapter sourced phenylacetone from the Oakland chapter in California and supplied them with the chemical.
